content.text: Final assembly checklist. Initial each line. Lines without initials are still binding.
Résumé, trait, kit, stats — picked. This page turns the picks into a sheet.
Stats. Write your five stats and their bonuses from the one bonus table on Stats & Growth. (The starting array itself is an open gap — flagged on Creation Overview.)
Skills. Mark the two Tier 1s your résumé granted and note each tier's feature pick (The Twelve Skills). Every other skill rolls untrained — The Two Rolls shows what that means.
Trait. Record both halves — the gift and the ledger line (Traits). Where it changes a number — Heavy Frame's +2 HP, Rift-Touched's Saturation floor, Survivor's scar — apply it now.

Kit. List your gear loadout; weapon and armor stats come from Weapons and Armor, ammo rules from Consumables & Ammo. Record your free entry augment — its effect is printed on its tree's page — and your technique (Techniques).
Credits. Your starting credits are your résumé line — see Résumés. Custom-kit contractors add any loadout budget they left unspent.

Derived numbers. Evasion comes from Edge and Reflex, capped by armor — see Attacks & the Shell Clock. Max Flesh HP comes from Grit — the formula lives on Stats & Growth. Free vent is your Grit bonus — see Charge & Saturation.

Tracks. Charge starts at 0. Saturation starts at 0 — Rift-Touched starts the floor at 5 (Charge & Saturation). Scars start at 0 — Survivor starts with 1 (Scars). And write the standing line on the sheet: it's as real as the gear.
everything on the sheet starts at zero. nothing stays there. that's the whole game.

content.label: STARTING STATE
content.text: Day one, every track sits at zero unless a trait says otherwise:
items: Scars 0 — Survivor starts with 1, carrying no debuff
Saturation 0 — Rift-Touched starts with a floor of 5

content.text: Day one is the cheap part. Where mission pay goes — stat training (Stats & Growth), augment installs (Trees Overview), techniques (Techniques) — and what a whole career costs are laid out on The Anchor Table and The Creditor Table.
Retain a copy of the completed sheet for your records. The corp retains the original. The original is the version that's true.
sign anyway. you were never going to read all of it, and they know that.
